title: Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates
subtitle: This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.
caption:
Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates

This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.

The chart displays five distinct trend lines tracking global education metrics from 1970 to 2024. The primary enrollment rate, represented by the top blue line, fluctuates between approximately 90% and 105% throughout the entire period, peaking slightly above the 100% mark around 2008 before settling near 102% in 2024. Below this, the green line for primary completion begins near 78% in 2000 and rises steadily to roughly 88% by 2024. The lower secondary completion rate, shown in purple, starts at 60% in 2000 and climbs to just under 80% by the end of the timeline. The orange line for secondary enrollment begins at 40% in 1970, dips slightly in the early 1980s, and then ascends consistently to reach approximately 78% in 2024, closely converging with the lower secondary completion rate. The lowest trajectory, tertiary enrollment in brown, starts at roughly 10% in 1970 and remains relatively flat until the mid-1990s, after which it accelerates upward to reach roughly 43% by 2024. The visual data indicates that while primary metrics have remained high and stable, secondary and tertiary enrollment and completion rates have shown significant long-term growth, particularly from the turn of the millennium onward.
